title: Suryavarman II | Biography & Facts | Britannica
highlights:
Angkor Wat, dedicated to Vishnu, was begun in the early years of Suryavarman’s reign and was not finished until after his death. Surrounded by a wall and a moat, the building is decorated with sculptures portraying Suryavarman as Vishnu;  ...    Suryavarman put his brother-in-law, Harideva, on the Cham throne, but Jaya Harivarman I deposed him and reclaimed that throne. In 1150 Suryavarman died in the midst of a new campaign against Champa, leaving his people exhausted by war and victimized by the once-subservient Chams, who eventually ravaged Angkor. This article was most recently revised and updated by Encyclopaedia Britannica.

Introduction Suryavarman II (ព្រះបាទសូរ្យវរ្ម័នទី២) (ruled c. 1113–1150 CE) was one of the most illustrious kings of the Khmer  ...    Rise to PowerSuryavarman II ascended to the throne after a period of internal strife within the Khmer Empire. Historical records, including inscriptions and bas-reliefs, suggest that he seized power by defeating his rival, Dharanindravarman I, in a dramatic battle that saw him mounting a war elephant to claim victory.
